topics.title: Semaglutide vs Tirzepatide vs Retatrutide: The GLP-1 Generation Gap
topics.angle: Head-to-head comparison of the three leading GLP-1 receptor agonists — mechanism differences (single, dual, triple hormone receptors), published trial weight-loss percentages, and what each generation signals about where obesity medicine is heading.

topics.title: Why Compounded Semaglutide Costs What It Costs
topics.angle: Transparent breakdown of what goes into the retail price of compounded semaglutide — USP 797 sterile compounding overhead, active ingredient sourcing, third-party testing, cold-chain shipping — and why the cheapest option is almost never the safest.

topics.title: The Tirzepatide Dosing Ladder: A Beginner Reference
topics.angle: Plain-language walkthrough of the standard tirzepatide titration schedule (2.5mg → 15mg), why slow escalation matters for GI tolerance, and what published trial data shows at each step.

topics.title: Hit a Weight Loss Plateau on GLP-1s? What the Research Says
topics.angle: Common reasons weight loss stalls around month 4-6 on GLP-1 therapy, what STEP and SURMOUNT extension trials show about long-term response curves, and lifestyle levers that show up in the data.

topics.title: BPC-157 and Tendon Repair: What the Research Actually Shows
topics.angle: Summary of the preclinical evidence for BPC-157 in tendon and ligament healing — angiogenesis mechanism via VEGFR2/Akt-eNOS, rodent model outcomes, and the current regulatory status.

topics.title: TB-500 vs BPC-157: Two Recovery Peptides, Two Different Jobs
topics.angle: Side-by-side on the two most-searched recovery peptides — TB-500 (thymosin beta-4 fragment) for cell migration and angiogenesis, BPC-157 for gut and connective tissue. Mechanisms, research base, and why some users stack them.

topics.title: GHK-Cu: The Copper Peptide Backed by 40 Years of Skin Research
topics.angle: The unusually long research history of GHK-Cu, Pickart's foundational work on tissue remodeling, and what the published data shows for skin barrier, wound healing, and hair follicle signaling.

topics.title: NAD+ Injection vs Nasal Spray: Route of Delivery Explained
topics.angle: Why NAD+ is delivered via injection or intranasal spray instead of oral — bioavailability data, onset and duration differences, and what each route is typically used for.

topics.title: MOTS-c: The Mitochondrial Peptide Everyone's Researching
topics.angle: Mitochondrial-derived peptide biology in plain language — how MOTS-c was discovered, what the metabolic research shows, and why longevity researchers are paying attention.

topics.title: Sermorelin vs HGH Replacement: Why the Pathway Matters
topics.angle: GHRH analog vs recombinant HGH — how sermorelin prompts the body's own pulsatile release, what that means for the feedback loop, and what published studies show on adult GH insufficiency.

topics.title: Tesamorelin and Visceral Fat: What the Clinical Data Shows
topics.angle: The clinical trial base for tesamorelin in reducing visceral adipose tissue, the FDA-approved use, and what the off-label research conversation looks like.

topics.title: How to Reconstitute Peptides: The Step-by-Step Reference
topics.angle: A careful reference on reconstitution — bacteriostatic water vs sterile water, gentle mixing vs shaking, dosage math for common concentrations, and common mistakes to avoid.

topics.title: Storing Peptides at Home: Temperature, Light, and Shelf Life
topics.angle: USP-aligned storage guidance for reconstituted and lyophilized peptides — refrigeration ranges, freezer considerations, light exposure, and how long common products stay stable after mixing.

topics.title: Subcutaneous Injection Technique for Peptides
topics.angle: Reference on clean SC injection technique — site rotation, needle gauge selection, pinch vs no-pinch, and disposal. Strictly educational reference content.

topics.title: How to Spot Counterfeit Peptides: A Consumer Reference
topics.angle: Red flags for counterfeit or research-grade peptides being sold for human use — no COA, no USP-compliant sterile compounding, overseas-only shipping, prices that undercut legitimate compounders by 80%+.

topics.title: What a Peptide Certificate of Analysis Actually Tells You
topics.angle: Reading a real COA — HPLC purity, mass spec identity confirmation, endotoxin testing, and sterility. What to look for and what to question.

topics.angle: The USP 797 chapter in accessible terms — why it exists, what cleanroom classifications mean, and why it matters for anyone buying injectable peptides.

topics.title: 503A vs 503B: The Two Lanes of US Compounding
topics.angle: The regulatory difference between 503A (traditional compounding on prescription) and 503B (outsourcing facility) — what each is designed for and where peptide products typically live.

topics.title: GLP-1 Side Effects: What the Trial Data Shows
topics.angle: What published phase 3 trial data reports on GI side effects with GLP-1 therapy, how titration affects the curve, and the research on tolerance over time.

topics.title: Peptide Stacking: An Educational Reference Overview
topics.angle: An educational look at why people discuss combining peptides — examples like GHK-Cu with BPC-157, NAD+ with sermorelin, or GLP-1 with glycine — and what the research conversation actually supports.

topics.title: Tirzepatide plus Glycine: The Research Context
topics.angle: Background on why glycine is being paired with GLP-1 products in some compounded formulations — glycine's metabolic research, sleep and glucose data, and what the combination is trying to address.

topics.title: Semaglutide plus NAD+: What the Combined Formulation Aims For
topics.angle: The research rationale behind combining semaglutide with NAD+ in a single compounded formulation — metabolic support during weight loss, mitochondrial biology, and what the published data does and does not say.

topics.title: Peptide Therapy: A Beginner Reference Guide
topics.angle: Entry-level reference for someone brand new to peptide therapy — what peptides are chemically, how they differ from small-molecule drugs, and how the current US compounding landscape works.

topics.title: Retatrutide Phase 3: What to Watch as the Triple Agonist Advances
topics.angle: Where retatrutide is in the FDA pipeline, what the phase 2 NEJM data showed on weight loss percentage, and what signals to watch in the ongoing phase 3 program.

topics.title: Oral Dissolving Tablets for Men's Health: Why the Format Matters
topics.angle: Why oral dissolving tablet formulations exist for sildenafil and tadalafil, what the bioavailability research shows vs standard tablets, and where combination formulations fit.

topics.title: Bacteriostatic Water: What It Is and Why It's Used
topics.angle: The chemistry of bacteriostatic water (0.9% benzyl alcohol), why it's the default reconstitution solvent for multi-dose peptide vials, and how it differs from sterile water for injection.

topics.angle: Why U-100 insulin syringes are the standard for most SC peptide use, the math connecting units to mg, and choosing between 0.3 / 0.5 / 1 mL barrels.

topics.title: The 2026 FDA Peptide Regulatory Landscape at a Glance
topics.angle: Quick-reference overview of where US peptide regulation stands — which peptides are on the FDA 503A bulks list, what the Pharmacy Compounding Advisory Committee is reviewing, and what's changed in the past 12 months.

topics.angle: What cold-chain shipping means for temperature-sensitive peptide products — ice packs vs gel, transit-time design, summer logistics, and why lyophilized (freeze-dried) product is more forgiving than reconstituted.

topics.angle: Why most peptides ship as lyophilized powder — stability shelf life, shipping logistics, storage flexibility — and what happens chemically during reconstitution.

topics.title: A Peptide Research Glossary for First-Time Readers
